sourceMovie.title: The Name of the Rose
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Name of the Rose for the same reason it broke through in 1986: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jean-Jacques Annaud keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Name of the Rose clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Name of the Rose clicked for you, The Silence of the Lambs (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Name of the Rose?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, The Silence of the Lambs, The Da Vinci Code—each shares DNA with The Name of the Rose while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Name of the Rose?
faq.answer: The Name of the Rose (1986) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Name of the Rose worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
